behavior: infra/config/values/common.yaml declares a top-level cluster_bootstrap list. Each entry has {name,namespace,manifest} plus optional {version,render,optional}. It seeds agent-sandbox (version v0.5.0rc1) and coredns-custom (render RESOLVE_RPI4_TAILSCALE_IP, optional:true). It is the single source of truth for cluster-wide component versions/render maps. (traefik is NOT present: its old branch was dead code, removed.)
verification: toolkit config validate && python3 -c "import yaml,sys; d=yaml.safe_load(open('infra/config/values/common.yaml')); ops={o['name'] for o in d['cluster_bootstrap']}; sys.exit(0 if {'agent-sandbox','coredns-custom'} <= ops else 1)"

behavior: A reusable toolkit primitive resolves RESOLVE_* placeholders via MagicDNS, substitutes in-memory, dry-runs server-side, then applies. The dead _get_traefik_config_path() is deleted and no RESOLVE_*-substitution shell remains in the Makefile (coredns/rpi4, uptime-kuma/rpi3, argocd/aws1 all migrated).
verification: ! grep -Rq '_get_traefik_config_path' toolkit/ && ! grep -Eq 's/RESOLVE_|RESOLVE_[A-Z0-9_]+/\\$' Makefile && python -m pytest toolkit -k 'render or cluster_bootstrap' -q

behavior: agent-sandbox v0.5.0rc1 is vendored at infra/k8s/cluster/agent-sandbox/manifest.yaml (CRD agents.x-k8s.io/v1beta1 + controller + RBAC + namespace), version-pinned and reproducible.
make sync-operators refreshes a vendored operator manifest deterministically from the SSOT-pinned version, for cluster_bootstrap entries that have a version (config entries like coredns are skipped).
After make deploy-k8s ENV=staging the agent-sandbox controller is Ready and the sandboxes.agents.x-k8s.io v1beta1 CRD is served on the staging cluster.
verification: KUBECONFIG=~/.kube/kubelab-staging-config kubectl get crd sandboxes.agents.x-k8s.io -o jsonpath='{.spec.versions[*].name}' | grep -qw v1beta1 && KUBECONFIG=~/.kube/kubelab-staging-config kubectl -n agent-sandbox-system rollout status deploy/agent-sandbox-controller --timeout=180s

behavior: iris SDD-034c Kubernetes adapter passes its conformance suite (Start/Stop/Status/Exec + sidecar) against the real staging cluster — the downstream validation this spec unblocks.
